title: Interfaces for durable entities should satisfy the restrictions
description: The recommended way to access Azure Durable Entities is through generated proxy objects with the help of interfaces. The following restrictions, during interface design, are enforced: Entity interfaces must be defined in the same assembly as the entity class. This is not detected by the rule.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects Azure Durable Entity interfaces with methods that violate restrictions (return values, out parameters)
logic.triggering_conditions: (Interface method returns non-void/non-Task) OR (Method has out/ref parameters)
logic.reasoning: Azure Durable Entities use proxy objects generated from interfaces. These interfaces have strict requirements: methods must return void or Task (not values), and cannot use out/ref parameters. Violating these restrictions causes runtime errors when the entity proxy is generated or invoked.

should_trigger: interface ICounterEntity { void GetValue(out int value); }
